title: grossularia microphylla
intro: Grossularia microphylla, also known as creeping thyme or dwarf thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by small, oval-shaped leaves and tiny pink or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Grossularia microphylla is believed to have various health benefits, including antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil to create teas, salves, or tinctures that can be used to support overall health and well-being.
